summary: Cryptic error spam when a script error happens too early

description.text: ### Tested versions 4.5 beta6 and likely earlier ### System information W10 ### Issue description This ```GDScript class_name BugTheme extends Theme @export var bug: Array[Shader] ``` causes It happens when you set the theme as project's default. Source is here: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/d5ad0556a2c04c50694b5c04dc1b3bf03ecd7113/scene/debugger/scene_debugger.cpp#L470-L471 I assume the script tries to send some error message too early. ### Steps to reproduce There might be other cases, but for the one above: - Make a new global Script that extends Theme - Add exported variable of `Array[Shader]` - Create instance of that class and assign some shader - Set the theme as `gui/theme/custom` project setting - Run the project ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [error2early.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22007614/error2early.zip)

summary: ThorVG should be updated

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.5 beta 6

### System information

Unrelated

### Issue description

There is an SVG rendering issue I've described here: https://github.com/thorvg/thorvg/issues/3750

The rendering issue itself is not relevant, what's important is that it seems to have been addressed in one of the latest ThorVG versions, as their https://thorvg.github.io/thorvg.viewer/ treats the problematic SVG correctly.

### Steps to reproduce

Make a file with the SVG from https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/SVG/Reference/Attribute/stroke-dasharray and drag it into the Godot editor, and increase the scale to something like 10 to be able to see it well. Notice how the bottom stroke isn't as it should be.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: WSL paths cause error ERROR: Condition "f.is_null()" is true

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable





### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - WSL: 2.5.10.0 - kernel: 6.6.87.2-1 - WSLg: 1.0.66 - MSRDC: 1.2.6074 - Direct3D: 1.611.1-81528511 - DXCore: 10.0.26100.1-240331-1435.ge-release - Windows: 10.0.26100.4946

### Issue description

If the project is inside a WSL path it leads to this error:
`  ERROR: servers/rendering/renderer_rd/shader_rd.cpp:512 - Condition "f.is_null()" is true`


### Steps to reproduce

Create a project in WSL in this path:
`\\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu-24.04\home\user\workspace\game\Godot_tutorials\godotintroduction`
Opening it from Windows.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Any minimal project in WSL will trigger this behaviour.

summary: Crash when exporting projects using command line

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

I haven't tested any other version.

### System information

Windows 10 - Godot v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### Issue description

If I export my project using the dialog in the editor, it's fine. But if I do it through command lines, Godot crashes during export. Strangely, the project is valid if it's small, but if it's big enough it doesn't seem to be fully exported.

The command line export I use: Godot.exe --path ../Game --export-release "Windows Desktop" ../Game.exe

### Steps to reproduce

Just execute the aforementioned command line export: Godot.exe --path ../Game --export-release "Windows Desktop" ../Game.exe

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A: reproducible with a brand new project.

summary: _gui_input is no more called after some time and unguarded_linear_insert errors are spaming while not using sort at all

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

I haven't tested any other version.

### System information

Windows 10, Godot v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### Issue description

All of a sudden, one of my Node no more receives _gui_input (I've checked and it's still connected to the signal and none of my set_input_as_handled are hit).
At the same time, I have a bunch (roughly 50) of unguarded_linear_insert errors (sort_array.h:255) in the console. I don't use custom sorting, I actually don't even use sort in my code.

### Steps to reproduce

That's the hard thing: It's random. It happens after some time of playing. I can't really give you any information on what's causing it.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I can send you my game, but it's far from minimal (and is more than 10MB). But if I have I'll do it, as it's preventing playing as of now.

Edit: CallStack: > godot.windows.editor.dev.x86_64.exe!CowData::get(__int64 p_index) Line 187 C++ ### Steps to reproduce Code: ```gdscript @abstract class Advertisement extends Object: static var _placement_id: Dictionary static func prepare(placement_id: String, loaded: Callable, shown: Callable, clicked: Callable, closed: Callable) -> void: pass @abstract class BannerAd extends Advertisement: static func |<--- ctrl + space + enter ``` ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

summary: UndoRedo history mismatch error when working with Resources that contain NodePath

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] ### System information Godot v4.4.1.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.6.0) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M1 Pro (Apple7) - Apple M1 Pro (8 threads) ### Issue description Hello I'm fairly new with Godot and I'm currently trying to leverage the editor and some script to configure characters in my game. In particular is the use of Resources in a `CharacterBody2D`, and how they store `NodePath`s. Specifically I noticed that if I'm trying to `@exports` for a specific `Resource` that contains an `@export_node_path` field, when I try to include the `CharacterBody2D` in another scene, the following error keeps popping up: ``` ERROR: UndoRedo history mismatch: expected 1, got 3. ERROR: UndoRedo history mismatch: expected 1, got 3. ``` Honestly I'm not sure why this happens, I didn't see any documentation that states that I can't use NodePath inside a resource, which leaves me scratching my head. It doesn't seem like a big issue, but it does clutter the Output and make it confusing to debug other problems. I left a video that shows how to reproduce the issue: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ee9dcd38-f5dd-48cc-9c67-c9091adc721a ### Steps to reproduce ## Create a Resource Script 1. Create a resource script that has the following: ```gdscript class_name ResourceTest extends Resource @export_node_path("Sprite2D") var test_path: NodePath ``` ## Creating Character Scene w/ Resource 1. Create a `CharacterBody2D` scene. 2. Add a `CollisionShape2D` to the scene. 3. Add a `Sprite2D` to the scene. 4. Add a `Node` to the scene . 5. Add a nested `Node` to the one added in previous step. 6. In the new nested `Node` attach a new script that looks like the following: ```gdscript extends Node @export var resource_test: ResourceTest ``` 7. In the Inspector window add a `NewResouceTest`. 8. In the `NewResourceTest` select the `Sprite2D` as the path value for `Test Path`. The Scene Tree should look something like: ## Adding the new CharacterBody2D to a separate Scene. 1. Create a new 2D Scene. 2. Add the `CharacterBody2D` that was previously created. 3. Add another Node to the scene 4. Move the `Character4Body2D` node in the scene tree. 5. You will see the following error in the output: ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) Project that can reproduce the issue: - [nodepath-resource-bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22002078/nodepath-resource-bug.zip)

summary: Unable to use animation player with animated sprite 2Ds

description.text:
### Tested versions

I only tested this in 4.4.1 stable official

### System information

Windows 11, I'm using an acer swift 14 Go, this doesn't seem to be a thing that would be dependent on specs though.

### Issue description

When trying to use an animation player to control what animation is playing in an animated sprite 2d it causes some unintended behavior. This is closer to an exploit than a bug. The fact that changing a sprite property opens up the sprite frames tab works weirdly when that property is being edited by the animation player. It causes the sprite frame tab to open when it goes over the keyframe but in the animation tab the cursor is binded to the mouse so you can't move the mouse without changing the animation.

### Steps to reproduce

create a animated sprite 2d with 2 or more animations and attempt to keyframe the current animation property in the keyframe. do that a second time and run the animation.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I don't have the time to do this but it seems so simple to reproduce. Sorry though.

summary: Stabilize viewport behavior under FSR2

description.text:
- Fixes #105509

Edit - Fix without the resizing is here:
 - #110119 if that is preferred (see comments). The alternative without the resizing does not have concerns for backporting, unlike this PR, in my opinion.

Below is the original message

---

When a subviewport using FSR2 upscaling falls below 64px on at least one dimension it produces this error spam:

> ERROR: Condition "p_job.uavMip[i] >= mip_slice_rids.size()" is true.
> Returning: FFX_ERROR_INVALID_ARGUMENT
>    at: execute_gpu_job_compute_rd
>    (servers\rendering\renderer_rd\effects\fsr2.cpp:418)

Additionally, it was possible through the scaling factor to have either dimension fall below `2px`, crashing the editor.

While testing using the MRP from the linked issue, I found that it was also possible to set the dimensions of the viewport above `16384`, which does not crash the editor but causes updating of the subviewport to stop (at least when rendering to a viewport texture) until the editor is restarted (or presumably until rendering of the subviewport is restarted somehow). It seems to me that the code intended to clamp sizes within a valid range but the enforcing was inconsistent, possibly because it assumes the viewport provided to `_configure_3d_render_buffers()` has a valid size, as the original code makes much more sense with that assumption.

Also, when the code falls back to Bilinear from FSR2, the scaling type is not re-evaluated, leading to it rendering with Bilinear scaling but still using jitter, which causes a lot of wobble, especially on smaller subviewport sizes.

This PR:

  * Enforces an upper limit of `16384` to subviewport dimensions

    - This upper limit is hardcoded in various parts of the rendering code and is a sensible value, considering most modern devices support this upper limit and few support higher, so I just added it as a raw value where relevant. I feel it is used so much it should be defined by the preprocessor but it's not a big deal at the moment. This fixes crashing the render thread by exceeding maximum texture limits through a scale factor above `1.0`.

  * Restores the proper scaling mode type after fallback to Bilinear. This fixes jitter that can occur due to fallback to Bilinear.

  * Ensures that the render size of a subviewport that uses FSR2 will always be of valid dimensions, preserving aspect ratio and also ensuring the target size is always equal to or larger than the render size, to preserve a scaling of `1.0` or less. This fixes the error spam and editor crash referenced in the linked issue.

    - This is done by first scaling up the render size so the smaller dimension is at least `2px`, then scaling it up again so the larger dimension is at least `64px`. The scaling is done on both dimensions proportionally on each pass so as to preserve the aspect ratio and prevent stretching. The scaling up is the minimal possible. The target size is then put through the same process to ensure it is always equal to or larger than the render size on both dimensions (so we're never accidentally downscaling, since this is not supposed to be done with FSR2).
 
      - This is a spreadsheet that repeats the same calculations for various viewport size dimensions, to test for edge cases and overall behavior. It also tests what would happen if we try to preserve the 3d scaling: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1xiGvhqp6tYgkyyENmOtAHOpoSXYu-0OmtalhVGiSE14/edit?usp=sharing

    - Scaling factor is not preserved due to this adjustment but I feel that the intent of the user to use FSR2 is to improve performance and render to a lower resolution, so it makes more sense to preserve the aspect ratio while also keeping it as small as possible, rather than trying to preserve the user-defined render scale. Trying to preserve the original scaling factor can result in the target size growing enormously, which defeats the purpose of using FSR2 on any scale other than `1.0`, and at a scaling of `1.0` all we need to do is make sure the dimensions are valid for the subviewport, which is already guaranteed due to the previous changes.

Possible concerns:

  * The changes don't fix FSR2 so that it won't choke on render sizes that are below 64px on both dimensions, nor does it address it crashing the editor when the size is below 2px on either of them. I'm not sure if this is due to FSR2's nature or if there is a bug somewhere but if it is the latter, this PR's fix will mask this error and make it harder to trigger, so likely harder to debug and fix in the future. Since the previous behavior can't be toggled back, anyone trying to fix the actual error might be in for a lot of trouble if they don't know about this adjustment.
  
    * I also never figured out why that specific error was occuring. I feel like it might be related to mipmap issues that occur in relation to FSR2 elsewhere but I didn't manage to find anything to prove or disprove this, so I gave up on investigating the mipmap detail.

  * Although the target size is adjusted, if the subviewport is set to `2x2`, the preview seems to show just 4 pixels being rendered, when the render size internally should be `64x64` due to the adjustment and so should the target size. I'm not sure what to make of this or if it's a concern but it feels like I may have misunderstood how this whole thing works and I've made the wrong changes. I went through this code with gdb several times but I think the rendering is done on a separate thread so I could only step through the render buffer creation but not what follows it up to the point of rendering (and I'm guessing it would take forever just to step through all that, given how often even `_configure_3d_render_buffers()` gets triggered even on the UI).







  * The new code contains multiple `if` statements and I'm unsure if I should write it differently to make branch prediction easier. It isn't a particular performance issue in most cases but it might be if someone is just using the slider in the editor, since every change requires going through this code. However, this new code only runs if the dimensions are too small, so for most cases it doesn't run anyway but it might cause failed branch predictions on the cpu. I'm uncertain about whether something needs to be done about this or not. I wrote things in a way that seemed nice to me but i'm not sure if that's the most clear and maintainable way to do it. I would also have liked to do this whole thing branchless, with just one fancy calculation but I couldn't figure out how to do it and even then, it might be a bad idea for maintainability. Plus, the clamps and max macros already run conditionals as it is. Fingers crossed for the compiler to guess correctly and optimise it.
  * I would have preferred to make a utility function to do this size adjustment, since it's done once for the render size and once again for the target size and it's the same calculation. It can be done without side-effects, so it would be a pure function and it would make the code more concise and thus easier to read, whereas now it is a block of ifs, but i have no idea where i would put this function or even if it should start with an underscore and things like that. It feels icky because I removed repetitive code only to introduce repetitive code of my own but I was unsure about what is preferred within the codebase.

possible questions:
   * "Why not just turn scaling to bilinear when going below 64x64 and be done with it?"
     * This is what I did at some point but FSR2 still has a much sharper look even at these low render sizes, which may be desirable. So I decided it wasn't good enough because there is some Legitimate Use Case (tm) for small viewports using FSR2 instead of bilinear
  * "Why not just clamp the render size?"
    * Because that can easily mess up the aspect ratio, which was obvious when I _did_ do this (it was what I tried first) and then went into the editor and played around with the dimensions. The crash and errors were impossible to trigger but once you fell below 64px on either dimension, you got a square render size which, when applied to a non-square viewport size, resulted in squashing and distortion which is very unlikely to feel expected by the user. So it's wasteful but also, wrong.

addendum:
  * Figuring out how this worked and having to decide how to fix it made me think that perhaps other adjustments need to be made but those are not decisions I can make alone, so I decide to open up proposals for them ~~but I'm unsure if I should make them in one proposal or separately~~:
    * godotengine/godot-proposals#13085
    * godotengine/godot-proposals#13084

Edit - Added link to spreadsheet that shows how the resizing code behaves from small viewports, high aspect ratios, up to large viewports.

Edit 2 - added link to alternative PR at the top

summary: Export variable not updating in custom resource when changed from external script

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in:  v4.5.beta6.mono

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta6.mono - Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS 24.04 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT (RADV NAVI32) - AMD Ryzen 9 7900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads) - 30.46 GiB memory

### Issue description

When using a custom resource, `@export` variables do not update in the remote view window when changed by script. Even after adding `@tool` to the resource, the value of `updateInt` does not change in the remote view window when running the scene.


Custom Resource:

```gdscript 
@tool
extends Resource
class_name NewResource

@export var updateInt: int = 0`
```
Resource referenced and changed by another script:

```gdscript 
extends Node2D

@export var resource: NewResource

func _ready() -> void:
	resource.updateInt = 99
	print("Curent Value: " + `str(resource.updateInt))
``` 

Output to console is `Curent Value:  99`  but the resource in the remote view still displays 0. This is not just a graphical glitch either. When I increase `updateInt` by one in the remote view using the arrows, the value does not jump to 100, it only goes to 1.

### Steps to reproduce

- Run scene in MRP
- Open remote view for Node2D
- Open `NewResource` in Node2D
- Value displayed for `updateInt` will be different from value reported in console 

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[MRP_ExportBug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22000252/MRP_ExportBug.zip)

summary: Clean up some things in shader editor code

description.text: This PR has some misc cleanups, such as renaming and organizing things. This is a prerequisite cleanup to PR #64596, I'm opening a separate PR for this change so it can be reviewed and merged in advance of the rest, making the changes in PR #64596 smaller and easier to read later. * In `shader.h` and `shader_types.cpp`, add comments noting that those need to be kept in sync. EDIT: And also add a DEV_ASSERT to ensure that they are kept in sync as @Calinou suggested. * In `ShaderEditorPlugin::edit`, rename `si` to `shader_include`, rename `s` to `shader`, add some comments, and check that the object trying to be edited is actually a shader. * In `ShaderEditorPlugin::_switch_to_editor`, check for null being passed. This will matter once it's possible for shader languages to arbitrarily plug into here, to avoid crashing if those languages have an error. * In `ShaderEditorPlugin::_make_script_list_context_menu`, make the validation language-agnostic. * In `ShaderCreateDialog::_validate_path`, rename `p` to `stripped_file_path`, rename `extension` to `file_extension`, and cache the shader type data in a `current_type_data` variable instead of re-getting it every time. * In `ShaderCreateDialog::_update_language_info`, give `.tres` a higher priority than `.res`, and avoid repeating the non-default-extension parts. * Move `ShaderCreateDialog` code to get the type icons into a new `_refresh_type_icons` function, and make it language-agnostic. The motivation behind this change is to avoid hard dependencies on specific types, and also to allow re-running this logic later, such as if shader languages are registered later on. The new `_refresh_type_icons` also fixes a bug where the ShaderInclude icon already present in the engine just wasn't being used by the dialog for some reason. Before, in master: After, with this PR:

summary: Add debug draw override to editor Game mode

description.text:
This allows overriding the debug draw mode used in the root viewport.

For example, this can be used to temporarily view the running project in wireframe/overdraw mode, which can be used to better diagnose certain issues.

Note that certain debug draw modes have no effect in Mobile/Compatibility due to renderer limitations, but this limitation is already present in the editor. Since we reuse the 3D editor's code for the advanced menu, all the tooltips and disabled items according to the current renderer are still in place.

- This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12866.

## Preview

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/14777a5a-46cc-4c4c-a8a3-7ee8f97d5579

## TODO

- [ ] Fix wireframe mode not working in Compatibility unless you call `RenderingServer.set_debug_generate_wireframes(true)` early enough in the project's lifecycle.
  - We could call this method automatically on startup when game embedding is enabled. Note that it has an impact on loading times and VRAM utilization, so maybe it should be an opt-in project setting.

summary: `EXC_BAD_ACCESS` when changing MetalFX scale with Compatibility

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.1
- Reproducible in 4.5 beta 5

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.6.1) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M4 Pro - Apple M4 Pro (14 threads)

### Issue description

I get a `EXC_BAD_ACCESS` crash when changing the `rendering/scaling_3d/scale` property in project settings when using Compatibility with MetalFX (Spatial):

``` C++
return driver->has_feature(p_feature);
```

I haven't worked with Xcode in over a decade, but I see this when I try and get info on `driver`... I think it's null?

```
Printing description of this->driver:
(RenderingDeviceDriver *) driver = <parent is NULL>
```

Here's the call stack:

```
#0	0x0000000105899d38 in RenderingDevice::has_feature at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/servers/rendering/rendering_device.cpp:7337
#1	0x000000010587bd04 in RendererViewport::_configure_3d_render_buffers at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/servers/rendering/renderer_viewport.cpp:161
#2	0x00000001058817f8 in RendererViewport::viewport_set_scaling_3d_scale at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/servers/rendering/renderer_viewport.cpp:1029
#3	0x00000001059b3ba0 in RenderingServerDefault::viewport_set_scaling_3d_scale at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/servers/rendering/rendering_server_default.h:708
#4	0x0000000103c6d710 in Viewport::set_scaling_3d_scale at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/scene/main/viewport.cpp:4870
#5	0x00000001036a2b68 in Node3DEditorViewport::_project_settings_changed at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/editor/scene/3d/node_3d_editor_plugin.cpp:3018
#6	0x00000001036eb51c in call_with_variant_args_helper<Node3DEditorViewport> at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/core/variant/binder_common.h:223
#7	0x00000001036eb474 in call_with_variant_args<Node3DEditorViewport> at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/core/variant/binder_common.h:337
#8	0x00000001036eb334 in CallableCustomMethodPointer<Node3DEditorViewport, void>::call at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/core/object/callable_method_pointer.h:103
#9	0x0000000106482780 in Callable::callp at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/core/variant/callable.cpp:57
#10	0x000000010686edf8 in Object::emit_signalp at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/core/object/object.cpp:1294
#11	0x00000001005a40a4 in Object::emit_signal<> at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/core/object/object.h:941
#12	0x000000010691928c in ProjectSettings::_emit_changed at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/core/config/project_settings.cpp:536
#13	0x000000010692d2f8 in call_with_variant_args_helper<ProjectSettings> at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/core/variant/binder_common.h:223
#14	0x000000010692d250 in call_with_variant_args<ProjectSettings> at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/core/variant/binder_common.h:337
#15	0x000000010692d110 in CallableCustomMethodPointer<ProjectSettings, void>::call at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/core/object/callable_method_pointer.h:103
#16	0x0000000106482780 in Callable::callp at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/core/variant/callable.cpp:57
#17	0x0000000106865198 in CallQueue::_call_function at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/core/object/message_queue.cpp:220
#18	0x000000010686559c in CallQueue::flush at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/core/object/message_queue.cpp:268
#19	0x0000000103c1b974 in SceneTree::process at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/scene/main/scene_tree.cpp:702
#20	0x00000001005cfc04 in Main::iteration at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/main/main.cpp:4802
#21	0x0000000100500530 in invocation function for block in OS_MacOS_NSApp::start_main() at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/platform/macos/os_macos.mm:1102
#22	0x0000000186a85ed8 in __CFRUNLOOP_IS_CALLING_OUT_TO_AN_OBSERVER_CALLBACK_FUNCTION__ ()
#23	0x0000000186a85dc0 in __CFRunLoopDoObservers ()
#24	0x0000000186a854d0 in __CFRunLoopRun ()
#25	0x0000000186a84a98 in CFRunLoopRunSpecific ()
#26	0x000000019252727c in RunCurrentEventLoopInMode ()
#27	0x000000019252a4e8 in ReceiveNextEventCommon ()
#28	0x00000001926b5484 in _BlockUntilNextEventMatchingListInModeWithFilter ()
#29	0x000000018a9a9a34 in _DPSNextEvent ()
#30	0x000000018b348940 in -[NSApplication(NSEventRouting) _nextEventMatchingEventMask:untilDate:inMode:dequeue:] ()
#31	0x000000018a99cbe4 in -[NSApplication run] ()
#32	0x00000001005001e8 in OS_MacOS_NSApp::run at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/platform/macos/os_macos.mm:1056
#33	0x0000000100576674 in main at /Users/allen/godot-dev/godot-src/platform/macos/godot_main_macos.mm:126
#34	0x00000001865fab98 in start ()
```

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a project with Compatibility rendering method
- Set `rendering/scaling_3d/mode.macos` to MetalFX (Spatial)
- Adjust `rendering/scaling_3d/scale`
- Crash

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

summary: Omni Shadows redrawn when object on unrelated layer moves

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproduced in version v4.4.1.stable.mono.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6109) - AMD Ryzen 9 5950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

Omni-lights have a Shadow Caster Mask option which allows you to choose which layers contribute to the shadow rendering. Visually this works correctly however I'd expect it to also take this into account when determining whether it needs to re-render the shadow.

Instead it is redrawing the shadow when any object moves within the bounding box of the light even if it is on a completely unrelated layer. This is a significant issue in my project as I'm using a lot of layers for a variety of different purposes including rendering 3D elements into a UI.

This also affects attempts to optimise within the main game world by removing certain mobile items from shadow casting, as it still redraws the shadows anyway.

I searched for existing issues but could not find one specific to this. Thanks.

### Steps to reproduce

Step 1. Set up a scene with a camera, omni-light with shadows enabled and some meshes to visually demonstrate the results.
Step 2. Set every layer option for all the objects above to Layer 1 only, especially the shadow caster on the omni light.
Step 3. Run and verify shadows are working correctly
Step 4. Using the Visual Profiler verify that the shadows are not being redrawn every frame (they shouldn't be as the scene is at rest)
Step 5. Add a script to rotate one of the meshes, verify that the shadows are now being redrawn both visually and in the profiler.
Step 6. Move the rotating mesh out of layer 1.
Step 7. Verify the shadow from this mesh is no longer present visually (this works correctly)
Step 8. Using the visual profiler, observe that the shadow is being re-rendered every frame at significant cost despite the mesh being on a layer not included in the shadow casting.

In the supplied MRP, there are two boxes one in layer 1 and one in layer 2, with an exported Rotate flag. As supplied the box in layer 2 only has rotation enabled, to demonstrate the issue. Turn off this rotate option and shadow re-drawing in the visual profiler will stop.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[ShadowLayers.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21990871/ShadowLayers.zip)

summary: Inspector does not update resource data if the data is imported from json.

description.text: ### Tested versions v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] ### System information MacOS Monterey 12.7.6 ### Issue description While I am working an addon: import data from json and save as resource file(.tres), the inspector shows the one which was loaded from the first time instead of the latest version which updated from json. I'd tried to delete (via Godot programatically and manually or terminal) and recreate the resource files, but the inspector's still showing the cached first loaded version. Data did updated if the changes are done via inspector. I have to reopen the godot to load the latest version resource files if I would like to update the resource data via json import. Or I have to open the resource file in terminal to confirm the data is updated. The settings from config file also do the same, the settings were updated while printing in output. Old settings were used while import. Until the addon was disabled, removed the config file in terminal, and then enable it again, settings will be updated. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Prepare the data from json (already in the zip file) 2. Import data via the importer 3. Load the resource data in inspector to confirm the data is imported 4. Change the resource data in json and save it 5. Import it again 6. Compare the data in terminal and inspector with json file PS: Importer UI is on the top right corner of Godot ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [testing-project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21992516/testing-project.zip)

summary: Add EditorScenePostImportPlugin::get_option_values

description.text:
Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/13068

In `EditorScenePostImportPlugin` we can currently retrieve option values with `get_option_value` but have no way of determining what the available options are. This PR adds a `get_option_values` method that returns the options Dictionary.

Sample project: 
[importertest2.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21986417/importertest2.zip)

Double click on the gltf in project root and hit import. You'll see an output print of `get_option_values()`.

summary: Editor freeze on project reimport when "Run physics on separate thread" is enabled (Godot 4.4.1, GT940M laptop)

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot version: 4.4.1 (stable, official build). 

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce 940MX (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8108) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-7200U CPU @ 2.50GHz (4 threads) 32G Ram

### Issue description

Issue description

When the project setting Physics > Run On Separate Thread is enabled, the editor freezes during project load (asset reimport). The freeze occurs right after:
Importing assets - 0%
Executing pre-reimport operations...
Preparing scenes for reload - 100%
Preparation done
At this point the editor hangs indefinitely and must be killed via Task Manager.
Disabling the "Run On Separate Thread" option allows the project to import and load normally.
This issue only reproduces on older hardware (GT940M laptop). On a newer PC with GTX1650 the same project works fine.
Seems like a race condition between physics server initialization and asset importer.
when running with verbose enabled, the log file is not created. Apparently the problem occurs before the logging process begins
However, the problem only appears when reimporting. If you do not delete the cache folder .godot from the project, then on this computer the project works perfectly with the physics in a separate thread.
Additional observation:
Once, instead of staying frozen forever, the editor eventually "unfroze" after ~10 minutes and finished reimporting successfully. However, this only happened once out of many tries, making the bug look like a deadlock or livelock in the threaded physics initialization. Normally it never recovers and has to be killed from Task Manager.


### Steps to reproduce

Create a new project.
**Go to Project Settings > Physics > Run On Separate Thread and enable it.**
Import  several GLB models
run project and stop
Close the project and Godot Engine
**Delete the .godot/ folder to force reimport.**
Open the project again.
→ The editor freezes during the import phase.

This happens in all my projects on this computer. The attached minimal project uses jolt physics but the problem does not depend on it. The same thing happens when using the godot physics engine. The key point is to enable the checkbox for running physics in a separate thread in the project settings.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[new-game-project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21985665/new-game-project.zip)

summary: Make shader editor menu position consistent between shader languages

description.text: ### Tested versions - Engine Version: 4.5.beta.mono.double ### System information Mac OS Tahoe / Windows 11 ### Issue description Attached are two images and two videos, one video that shows the behavior in godot, and one video that shows the default behavior in unity (and the desired behavior.) We have two animations, a walk cycle, with 30 frames, and a jog cycle, with 24 frames. These animations should blend together fine, as the pacing is the same if you look at them with normalized time, which godot does not, despite having the "Sync" checkbox enabled on the BlendSpace. Godot seems to shorten the longest clip, in this case the walk cycle, which causes the walk to speed up drastically, and it still does not fix the "stumbling" in the 0.5 blend of the animations. Incorrect behavior from Godot https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8a1fbc53-6fa0-471a-a6c6-93e635e77aee Expected behavior in Unity https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b9cf96d9-2fe4-47e8-8958-1b5d8cef4215 ### Steps to reproduce - create 2 animations at 30 fps, one with 24 frames and one with 30 - add them to a blend space - check the "sync" box - one gets sped up, and they're still not synced. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

summary: Fix filtered objects getting unselected after reselection and certain properties change

description.text:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/code_style_guidelines.html
-->

This is a somewhat weird issue that I can only reliably reproduce in certain circumstances but sometimes can reproduce when just changing transforms for instantiate scenes. The fix is to suppress the filter removal unless the filter changes, to allow reselection without issues.

An example (note the conveyor getting unselected after I change the size property):

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/721ff05a-1372-422d-a2dc-f68f927c46dd

With this PR the conveyor remains selected.

summary: After checking "top_level" in Bone2D, the transformation error occurred

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.5.beta6

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta6.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 960M (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7283) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-6700HQ CPU @ 2.60GHz (8 threads) - 15.83 GiB memory

### Issue description

Why should I check top_level in Bone2D?

Because I want to use the RemoteTransform2D node to pass only the rotation value from the parent skeleton to the current skeleton, this operation is very common when making animations in spine software. In some cases, when we create animations, we do not want the child skeleton to inherit the rotation or scaling of the parent skeleton. Checking "top_level" allows you not to inherit the transformation of the parent node.





How do I expect to play a role?

When I select top_level, rotating the parent skeleton will not drive the child skeleton and the Polygon2D mesh to rotate. Whether this Polygon2D grid has weights or not.





What doesn't work?

When I select top_level, the child bones do not appear to follow the rotation when rotating the parent bone, and the Sprite2D nodes of the child bone nodes and the Polygon2D nodes without bone weights do not follow the rotation. However, Polygon2D nodes with skeletal weights will still rotate. This can be found from the project.

Polygon2D nodes in the skeleton are simultaneously affected by the transformation of the skeleton itself and the transformation of weights. I think this is unreasonable. Polygon2D with weights should not be affected by the transformation of the skeleton itself.

### Steps to reproduce

Reproduce the steps

Create 2-3 levels of child nodes bone2D. Select top_level for the lowest-level bone2d node and move the parent node to view the grid effect

In the demo project, you can try rotating the root bone, bone2 bone and bone bone respectively. It can be found that only when the bone bone rotates will all three child nodes rotate. When rotating the root bone or bone2 bone, the weighted grid of the baoxiang4 node will rotate, while the other two nodes will not rotate.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[demo.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21963756/demo.zip)

summary: Jolt physics freeze from maximum jobs being exceeded in non-threaded web export

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5.beta6.
- Not reproducible in 4.5.beta5, earlier 4.5 betas, or 4.4.1-stable.

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta6 - Kubuntu 24.04.3 LTS 24.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 970 (nvidia; 550.163.01) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-4790K CPU @ 4.00GHz (8 threads) - 15.56 GiB memory

### Issue description

When using the Jolt physics system, and running in the browser, the game will freeze after a short time with the error:
"WARNING: Jolt Physics job system exceeded the maximum number of jobs. This should not happen. Please report this. Waiting for jobs to become available..."
This freeze only occurs when thread support is disabled. It will also not occur when running with the editors' built in player - It must be running in the browser through remote deploy, or by running the exported project.

This occurs when a CharacterBody3D is calling move_and_slide(), and RigidBody3D is calling move_and_collide() or rigid bodies are otherwise moving, including through gravity.
In most instances this freeze will occur during the 120th physics frame.
The only instance it occurs later is in the provided forces example where the rigid body is not moving, in which case it can appear to be delayed until input is provided and the cube starts moving.

The freeze occurs when the project is running in both firefox and google chrome.

I have also tried to reproduce the issue in the editor player by setting the setting "threading/worker_pool/max_threads" to 1, but this does not cause the issue to appear.

When the freeze occurs it locks up the entire tab, but only that tab. Refreshing the tab will not work until the browser detects the tab is frozen and you hit stop.


### Steps to reproduce

- Create a new project
- Set "physics/3d/physics_engine" to Jolt Physics
- set "physics/common/physics_ticks_per_second" to 20, to give time to open the browser console before freeze occurs
- Restart editor

- Make a new 3D scene
- Add a RigidBody3D with CollisionShape3D as child with its shape set to any shape.

- Add a default Web Export preset
- Run scene through remote deploy
- Open the console in web browser.
- In a few seconds, the error will show up and the tab will freeze.

I have attached an export of the web log that occurs when following these steps. The script terminated line comes after clicking stop when the browser recognizes the tab is frozen.

[console-export-2025-8-24_20-1-53.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21960748/console-export-2025-8-24_20-1-53.log)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I have included two projects. The MRP follows the exact steps given in Steps To Reproduce, and the other project has a few more instances of the bug. 
This extra example project is set to the scene which applies forces with input, but otherwise is motionless. This scene is different than the others, as it is the only one where the freeze can occur after 120 physics frames. The cube can be moved with the arrow keys.

[MRP.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21960816/MRP.zip)

[A Few More Examples.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21960818/A.Few.More.Examples.zip)

summary: Fix hang when importing blender files in headless mode

description.text:
Godot currently tries to open a dialog box in headless mode then waits forever for it to complete. Instead just fail straight away when in headless mode.

Fixes #100122

summary: Fix tree to update size with scrollbars disabled

description.text:
When scrollbars are disabled, tree size is dependent on item sizes, so this adds missing checks for when to resize. The side effect of the bug was that scrollbars could incorrectly appear when items change at runtime.

Fixes #105967
Fixes #106609
Also fixes the root cause of the bugs, so https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/106038 will not be needed.

Two things to note about `Tree` and the disable scrollbar properties:
1. Scroll bars are implicitly hidden depending on the minimum size (see `Tree::update_scroll_bars()`)
2. When a scrollbar is disabled, that min size is tied to item size (see `Tree::get_minimum_size()`)

The missing part was updating sizes if any scrollbar is disabled when a change could change content size (modifying items, adding items, removing items, adding columns) so that the `Tree` updates to the new size and keeps scrollbars hidden.

In the original bug report (#105967), starting `scroll_horizontal_enabled` and `scroll_vertical_enabled` `false` meant that there's be no size updates since there's none triggered from item changes, thus scrollbars appear once the content gets too large. The correct sizing behavior is only seen when transitioning from `true` to `false` since that requests a min size update via changing the property.

In the secondary bug report (#106609), `scroll_horizontal_enabled` and `scroll_vertical_enabled` are going from `true` (editor time) to `false` (runtime in the script) so the observed behavior is actually correct when starting `true`. The real issue is that the tree doesn't become super large when starting `false` since starting `false` and going from `true` to `false` should have the same appearance. This was pretty much the same cause as the original issue.

I've also made a small tester for adding/removing items and toggling various settings:
[tree tester.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21959797/tree.tester.zip)
Note that the tester itself doesn't do proper index checks for things like removing when there are no children to remove. It was just something quick to modify the tree without having to update the script constantly.

<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/code_style_guidelines.html
-->

summary: Crash when auto-filling abstract method

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5.beta6.mono

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta6.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8097) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-10900K CPU @ 3.70GHz (20 threads) - 47.86 GiB memory

### Issue description

I have a script inheriting from an abstract class that requires an abstract method to be implemented. The script does not contain an implementation. Typing `func p` brings up an autofill suggestion for perform_behavior(). (It may be worth noting the autofill suggestion includes a comment following the method). Hitting "enter" to use the suggestion crashes the editor.

### Steps to reproduce

```
## Abstract class, item_behavior_base.gd
@abstract class_name ItemBehaviorBase extends Resource

@abstract func perform_behavior(...args:Array)

## Context for the behavior to operate within.
var context
```
```
## Subclass, BVR_TEST_DebugPrint.gd
extends ItemBehaviorBase
## Typing "func p" and accepting the autofill suggestion, "func perform_behavior(...args:Array) ## Context for the behavior to operate within." crashes the editor.
```
I imagine there is an issue with the autocomplete misreading the abstract function declaration since it doesn't have a `:` at the end of the line, but that's just a guess.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A


EDIT: May be the same as #108639 (which did not come up the first time I searched to see if it had been reported already, apologies if it's a duplicate).

summary: One shot particles disable themselves

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4.1 stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8097) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-11800H @ 2.30GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

When using CPUParticles2D and checking the one shot option, I am expecting for the particle to play once ingame and then stop. However for me the particle plays in the editor (preview) and then the emitting property gets disabled so it wont even play ingame unless i program it to. Whats funny is that if I play the game before the editor finishes it, it works as expected ingame, turning it into a quick time event

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5aaccf3a-60eb-4b84-876f-d6c8470d4041


### Steps to reproduce

Create CPUParticles2D, enabled one shot and try playing

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Windows export reports wrong resolution based on main monitor's Scaling value

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in : 4.4 stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable (d127a0434) - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7680) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-9700K CPU @ 3.60GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

For Windows exports, games will report wrong resolution once moved to another monitor with a different scaling value (in Windows settings) from the _main monitor_, **even if** the target monitor has 100% scaling.

For example, if using `get_window().size`  : 
- Game starts on the main monitor (1440p, 125% scaling). Returned size is **2560x1440** : actual resolution, scaling ignored.
- Game is moved to the second monitor (1080p, 100% scaling). Returned size is **2400x1350**: This is 1080p + 25%, because the main monitor's scaling seem to be considered as the base. **This is the case even if the game is configured to start on this monitor from the beginning.**

This causes the game to look terrible, especially for pixel art games with integer scaling. (letterboxing + blur)

If project settings "**allow_hidpi**" is disabled, the problem is _reversed_: 
- Screen 2 now shows **1920x1080** correctly.
- Screen 1 becomes **2048x1152** (1440p -25%) and look terrible.

I have searched through a lot of DPI issues on the tracker, but none seem to be for this specific problem.

The ideal behavior (for pixel art games at least) would be for both screens to report their actual resolution.
This is possible by editing the App Manifest for the exported .exe. Taking the default manifest for a Godot exported .exe and replacing this section : 
```
<windowsSettings xmlns:ws2="http://schemas.microsoft.com/SMI/2016/WindowsSettings">
    <ws2:longPathAware>true</ws2:longPathAware>
</windowsSettings>
```
With the following:
```
<windowsSettings xmlns:ws2="http://schemas.microsoft.com/SMI/2016/WindowsSettings" xmlns:ws1="http://schemas.microsoft.com/SMI/2005/WindowsSettings">
    <ws2:longPathAware>true</ws2:longPathAware>
    <ws2:dpiAwareness>PerMonitorV2</ws2:dpiAwareness>
    <ws1:dpiAware>true/pm</ws1:dpiAware>
</windowsSettings>
````
Will tell Windows to let the application handle scaling, which leads to all monitors reporting the correct resolution.

I believe support for doing this without external tools should be added, either as a toggle on export, or as default behavior if this is also beneficial for non pixel art games.


PS. This happens in the editor too, but it can be worked around by right clicking the .exe file and checking the "let application handle scaling setting", so this is less of an issue. For actually shipping a game, it's pretty bad to have to tell every player to do that.

### Steps to reproduce

- Set up monitors in Windows Settings : System : Display, so that the main monitor has a different scaling value from the others.
- Launch any Godot Project.
- Project will run at the wrong resolution if not on the main monitor.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[minimum-resolution-bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21957656/minimum-resolution-bug.zip)
(The project just displays the current window size in a Label)
- Set up a different Scaling value for the main monitor in Windows Settings : System : Display
- Run project
- Move running project to another window (shortcut: Windows key + shift + right arrow)
- Displayed resolution will be wrong
- If not, try exporting the project to a .exe before running (you might have fixed it prior in your Godot editor)

summary: Popup: Reset state on visibility change

description.text:
Fixes #109817.

The original logic was never called for certain platforms, leaving the hide reason unset and causing weird semi-random issues with the few users of that internal API, such as when renaming nodes in the scene tree editor.

---

On a related note, it looks like there's a general inconsistency on what happens to the focus of a window with `FLAG_POPUP`. The documentation says that when this flag is enabled the window will "exclusively receive all input, without stealing focus from its parent" yet, due to IME issues, focus requests got eventually added to [Windows](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/77977), [MacOS](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/78029), and [X11](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/72497), although from the ticket above it looks like focus events might still not be sent properly for the latter either.

That said I'm still reasonably sure, judging from the users of `hide_reason` and `popped_up`, that the intent of the `NOTIFICATION_WM_WINDOW_FOCUS_IN` handler was indeed to reset everything each time the window shows up and not only when it gets keyboard focus.

summary: CharacterBody2D move_and_slide() treats wall corner as a floor, preventing the CharacterBody2D from falling

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in 4.0.stable, 4.1.stable, 4.2.stable, 4.3.stable, 4.4.stable, 4.4.1.stable, 4.5.beta6. ### System information Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1050 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-8400 CPU @ 2.80GHz (6 threads) ### Issue description When positioning a ```CharacterBody2D``` and another collision object like ```StaticBody2D``` or a ```TileMap``` tile with a collision shape in a particular way: (the green square is a ```CharacterBody2D``` and the blue square is a ```StaticBody2D```, the grid squares are the 1 pixel big) and moving the ```CharacterBody2D``` with ```move_and_slide()``` while applying gravity to it, ```move_and_slide()``` treats the collision with the wall (in this case the left edge of the ```StaticBody2D```) like a floor collision and prevents the ```CharacterBody2D``` from falling: while having ```is_on_floor()``` return false. ### Steps to reproduce - Create a ```CharacterBody2D``` and add to it a ```CollisionShape2D``` with shape ```RectangleShape2D```. - Attach to the ```CharacterBody2D``` the a script with the following movement code: ```gdscript extends CharacterBody2D const GRAVITY := 24 const VELOCITY_Y_MAX := 600 func _physics_process(_delta: float) -> void: velocity.y = clamp(velocity.y + GRAVITY, -VELOCITY_Y_MAX, VELOCITY_Y_MAX) move_and_slide() print(is_on_floor()) return ``` - Create a ```StaticBody2D``` and add to it a ```CollisionShape2D``` with shape ```RectangleShape2D```. - Position the ```StaticBody2D``` such that its left (or right) edge is on the same line of the right (or left) edge of the ```CharacterBody2D```, and its top edge is (at least) two pixels below the bottom edge of the ```CharacterBody2D```. - Run the scene. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [minimal_reproduction_project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21955784/minimal_reproduction_project.zip)

summary: RemoteTransform2D scene tree order among siblings affects behavior

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7640) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-4790 CPU @ 3.60GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

When adding Sprite2Ds to a Skeleton2D using RemoteTransform2Ds that are placed after the Bone2D child of the respective Bone2D, it results in the offsets lagging behind and the sprites ending up all over the place.
It's especially noticeable after moving (or halting) fast.

E.g.:
This works as expected:

```
Skeleton2D
>UpperArmBone2D
>>RemoteTransform2D
>>LowerArmBone2D
...
```

This leads to the Sprite2D lagging behind, overextending, and not halting in the correct location/rotation:

```
Skeleton2D
>UpperArmBone2D
>>LowerArmBone2D
>>RemoteTransform2D
...
```

I wouldn't expect the order among siblings to affect the RemoteTransform2D's transform, nor the transform it passes on, in any way.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a Skeleton2D using RemoteTransform2D to control render order of the Sprite2Ds and change the child index of the RemoteTransform2D. If it comes before the Bone2D, it bugs out. 

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[remotetransformweirdness.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21954707/remotetransformweirdness.zip)

summary: Submitting text with LineEdit temporarily breaks input

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.5.beta6.official [d5ad0556a]
v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.21025.1024) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-11900F @ 2.50GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

as the title states, submitting text with LineEdit disables input and hides the text cursor (while staying visually focused) until submitting a second time or clicking on the LineEdit. this is wasnt a previous issue in 4.3

### Steps to reproduce

1. write in a line edit
2. press enter
3. notice you cant write again without refocusing/resubmitting

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[lineedit_test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21953819/lineedit_test.zip)
